rationale: The two papers are tightly aligned in topic and design: both examine cause-related marketing promotions in consumer product contexts using multiple experiments and moderator frameworks. The cited 2011 paper provides moderator/boundary-condition evidence (identity, product type, charity efficiency) for donation-based promotions, which is plausibly a substantive theory/background source for the citing 2012 paper’s moderator-focused donation amount–WTP model. Given this clear conceptual overlap and no evidence of a purely incidental mention, this pair should be kept for full relation judgment.
